channel.title: Posts on Blog of Jason Meridth
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Blog of Jason Meridth

item.title: Gocode Vim Plugin and Go Modules
item.description: Update: My friend Eli Uriegas let me know that you don’t need gocode anymore since vim-go has autocompletion.

item.title: SSH - Too Many Authentication Failures
item.description: Problem I started seeing this error recently and had brain farted on why.

item.title: Clear DNS Cache In Chrome
item.description: I’m blogging this because I keep forgetting how to do it.

item.title: Create Docker Container from Errored Container
item.description: When I’m trying to “dockerize” an applciation I usually have to work through some wonkiness.

item.title: Unable To Access Mysql With Root and No Password After New Install On Ubuntu
item.description:
This bit me in the rear end again today. Had to reinstall mysql-server-5.7 for other reasons.

You just installed mysql-server locally for your development environment on a recent version of Ubuntu (I have 17.10 artful installed). You did it with a blank password for root user. You type mysql -u root and you see Access denied for user 'root'@'localhost'.

item.title: New Job
item.description: Well, it is a new year and I’ve started a new job.

item.title: Docker Daemon Error When Running Docker Compose
item.description: TL;DR Make sure you don’t have any old mounted volumes around if you see the error below.

item.title: Lenovo Thinkpad - Swap ctrl and fn keys
item.description: I just got a new laptop. It is a Lenovo Thinkpad X1 Carbon 5th Gen.

item.title: Ubuntu - set caps lock to escape
item.description: I just got a new laptop and had to google again on how to set caps lock key to escape (I’m a Vim user).

item.title: Cleanup Docker
item.description: I keep having friends who have experienced the no space left on device when trying to build images.

item.title: OnePlus 5
item.description: I’ve had a Nexus 6 for the last 2 years and was finally due for a phone upgrade.

item.title: Mapping Caps Lock to Esc Is Native to OSX Now
item.description: I have been using Seil for a few years now on OSX to map Caps Lock to Esc.

item.title: Details HTML Section In Github Issues and Gists
item.description: I recently became aware of using the <details></details> and <summary>...</summary> tags in Github issues and Gists.

item.title: Stop and Remove All Docker Containers
item.description: Command remove all docker containers: docker stop '$(docker ps -a -q)' && docker rm '$(docker ps -a -q)'

item.title: Get Repository Root Path
item.description: I needed to find a way to get the path to the root of my Git repository.

item.title: Vim Tagbar Plugin
item.description: I found the tagbar Vim plugin last night and finally tried it out this morning.

item.title: Information Shown After Logging Into Ubuntu
item.description: Afer you log into an ubuntu instance you’ll usually see something like this:

item.title: Other People's Code
item.description: TL;DR We are all continually improving. Don’t forget that. Recently I’ve been hearing more and more developers say stuff like “Wow, what was this person thinking when they wrote this?

item.title: How I Setup My Local Python Environment
item.description: I’ve had a few people ask me lately how I setup my local python environment.

item.title: Meta keys with iTerm and Weechat
item.description: Found this tip today about how to ensure your iTerm console sends +Esc to weechat for the meta keys to work properly.

item.title: Using PushBullet for Notifications in Android/Chrome
item.description: So I recently got my weechat (IRC) notifications coming to my android via Pushover.

item.title: Studying for RHCSA/RHCE Exams

item.title: Copying Text With Mouse In TMUX
item.description: TL;DR Hold down shift key and select text with left mouse button.

item.description: Today I needed to scan something on my Canon MX450 series printer from my laptop running Ubuntu 14.

item.description: The following command restarted audio for me in Ubuntu after my microphone on my headphones stopped working.

item.title: I Finally Have Linux As My Native OS
item.description: I finally replaced OSX on my Mac with Ubuntu 14.04 LTS.

item.title: Why I Blog
item.description: I’ve been asked recently why I blog. My answer is the same since I started blogging back in 2006.

item.description: Recently I had issues connecting to Github because of some firewall restrictions outside my control.

item.title: Where I Go To Choose An Open Source License
item.description: I go to choosealicense.com to refresh my memory on open source licenses.

item.description: I’ve been asked this a few times and have had to do it lately a few times.

item.title: How to Run a Single Test With Tox
item.description: I have to run a single python test once in a while and keep forgetting how to do it with Tox.

item.title: Using sed and regex to Remove Prefix on Filenames
item.description: I used wp2md recently to generate markdown files out of my wordpress posts so that I could migrate to a static blog generator (like Nikola, the one I chose to use on this blog).

item.title: Consolidated My Blogs
item.description: I finally consolidate my blogs that were located at: http://jason.

item.title: Using Default Scope and Unscoped in Rails
item.description: I recently had the need to add a deleted flag to a model in a Rails project.

item.description: A couple times now I’ve forgotten to render the DOCTYPE in my layouts on my web apps.

item.title: Run a Specific RSpec Spec
item.description: Update: You can specify a line number to run in your spec if you’re wanting to run a specific spec.

item.title: Get Sinatra and Shotgun gem working on Vagrant
item.description: TL;DR shotgun -o 0.0.0.0 config.ru You have to tell shotgun to use 0.

item.title: Install and Setup PostgreSQL on New Ubuntu VM
item.description: Install PostgresSQL and the dev package: sudo apt-get install postgresql libpq-dev Reference Cheat Sheet after installation

item.title: Set VIM as Default Editor
item.description: Environment: Ubuntu sudo update-alternatives --config editor OUTPUT: There are 4 choices for the alternative editor (providing /usr/bin/editor).

item.description: I’m putting this here so that I have it saved. I’ve done this many times and until I automate it with a Puppet or Chef script….

item.title: TMUX Cheat Sheet
item.description: Install TMUX sudo apt-get install tmux personal config file is at ~/.

item.title: No More Password Prompts When Issuing Sudo Commands
item.description: Edit the sudoers file: sudo visudo Add or edit the following line to be:

item.title: Use Git Grep to Replace Strings in Files in Your Git Repository
item.description: TL;DR On Linux: git grep -l 'original_text' | xargs sed -i 's/original_text/new_text/g' On Mac:

item.title: Renew DHCP Lease on Ubuntu VM After Moving Between Wireless Networks
item.description: TL;DR sudo dhclient -r [eth0] &amp;&amp; sudo dhclient [eth0] I add that command as an alias to my ~/.

item.title: Double Exclmations !! In Ruby
item.description: Today I hit this in a Ruby example online: def role?

item.title: Has And Belongs To Many Join Table Name Must Be In Alphabetical Order
item.description: TL;DR Read this from the Rails Guides Today I created an explicit join table between two models because each had has_and_belongs_to_many, as I’ve done many time before.

item.description: .bash_profile is sourced everytime you login to a bash shell either locally on the terminal or remotely via ssh

item.title: Testing Mail From the Command Line on Ubuntu
item.description: Install mailutils sudo apt-get install mailutils Send some test piped into the mail command

item.title: Using Latest Version of Git on Ubuntu
item.description: Whenever you do sudo apt-get install git-core on Ubuntu you will only get git version 1.

item.description: I use the command line with Git. I do this because I type faster than I use my mouse.

item.description: PROBLEM: You do not want to issue pull requests from your fork’s master branch on Github.

item.title: Twitterfeed.com killed my Google Reader
item.description: We recently created a TwitterFeed.com account for LosTechies. What this service does is query your RSS feed and whenever a new post is created, it will tweet the blog entry information.

item.description: UPDATE: Make sure your user account is a member of the vboxusers account.

item.title: Dell Dimension 3000 - remove hard drive
item.description: **Precursor: My MonoRail posts will continue soon, but I’m moving my office and have also had the “tech support” calls from friends and family lately, hence the OS, hardware posts.

item.title: Installing 2 CruiseControl services/web dashboards on 1 server/PC
item.description: Why? I am currently hosting CruiseControl for an open-source project and I don’t want my personal projects to show up on their web dashboard.

item.title: .NET InternalsVisibleTo attribute == C++ Friend Assemblies
item.description: To all developers who haven’t heard of this, there is an ability to have a project, say a test project, to have access to another project’s Internal classes.

item.title: Apple's new product - iBrick
item.description: This is what happens to your iPhone if you opened it up for use with other carriers.

item.title: Ubuntu 7.10 (Gutsy Gibbon) BETA released yesterday - final release in October
item.description: Ubuntu 7.10 released yesterday. Go here to download and help Canonical with beta testing.

item.title: Be kind to your testers
item.description: Jeremy Miller posted about Testers being pigs. I have learned through my current position that he is completely correct.

item.title: MonoRail
item.description: This post was decided upon after watching Hammet’s screencast on using attribute-based validation in MonoRail.

item.title: Domain Model Overuse post by David Laribee on CodeBetter
item.description: David Laribee recently posted on Domain Model Overuse and my colleague Joe Ocampo (AgileJoe) responded.

item.title: Ruby on Rails - WOW!
item.description: Okay, I’ve been hearing about Ruby and even went through the “Try Ruby” on hobbix walk-through.

item.title: MonoRail HotSwap
item.description: Ayende (pronounced A-yend (no eh at the end) - verified in this post after hearing Colin pronounce it) posted on an idea he has - MonoRail HotSwap and Colin Ramsay did a screencast of MonoRail HotSwap.

item.title: Forget Subversion's blame, we need the Microsoft's WSYP program (joke)
item.description: Originally posted here, where I found it. Comments jssingh: This is awesome!

item.title: Castle 1.0 Release Candidate 3 is out
item.description: Hammet posted that RC3 is out. For those of you who were on RC2 and hesitant to use the trunk, this is great news.

item.title: Another reason to love FaceBook
item.description: Check out this video Comments Joe Ocampo: I can’t believe you subjected me to that!

item.title: Agile Cheat Sheet
item.description: Excellent post by Jimmy Bogard. Definitely add him to your feeds if you don’t have him yet.

item.title: Brad Abrams hints (again) on Microsoft MVC framework for ASP.NET and Phil Haack will head it up
item.description: Brad Abrams, Group manager of the .NET Framework, posted today announcing the Microsoft hiring of Phil Haack, core SubText blogging engine developer.

item.title: Monorail
item.description: UNIT TESTING After the past 2 1/2 posts I’ve migrated my unit tests to use the BaseControllerTest abstract class that originated from the Eleutian guys, but is now a part of the Castle MonoRail trunk download.

item.title: Good find - software development videos
item.description: The videos can be found here. Names in videos include:

item.title: Skype + Yugma + Logitech Premium USB Headset 350 = Remote Pair Programming
item.description: My co-worker, Nelson, and I did some remote pair programming on a side project this weekend.

item.title: For those annoyed with having to add the public keyword to your new classes....
item.description: Read the following blog entry from David Hayden. This teaches you how to update the class template that Visual Studio 2005 uses to create new classes.

item.title: Agile Development
item.description: Currently I’m working on a team of 8 developers and have been practicing Agile for just under 2 years now.

item.title: Upgrade DotNetNuke from 4.03.03 to 4.05.05 and AlamoCoders
item.description: I found this post and followed it to the “T” upgrading a DNN site to 4.

item.title: Code snippets in blog entries, HELP!
item.description: UPDATE: Problem solved. Had to uninstall VSPaste plug-in first and uninstall the Code Snippet plug-in, restart, and then re-install.

item.title: Microsoft FolderShare
item.description: Today I was working with a co-worker, Nelson Montalvo, and talking to him about my file transfer woes with moving this site and other sites to a new server.

item.title: Monorail
item.description: One of the first things I should have posted: How It Works

item.title: Monorail
item.description: LAYOUTS UPDATED: Used the Code Snippet plug-in Layouts are like master pages or template pages in Adobe DreamWeaver.

item.title: I didn't think websites still required a certain platform...
item.description: rant Precursor: I’m not as much anti-Microsoft as I used to be, but this re-ignited the flame.

item.title: Castle API available now
item.description: Hammet has announced that the Castle API is now available online.

item.title: Monorail
item.description: UPDATE: I am using the Castle MonoRail project trunk code, NOT RC2 REASONS:

item.title: SVNRepository.com ROCKS!!
item.description: To all developers, If you are located for co-located Subversion hosting, I’ve now looked at, tried/purchased an account with SvnRepository.

item.title: Formatting the RSS Module in DotNetNuke to handle HTML and other formatting tips
item.description: I’m currently in the process of moving the Alamo Coders website to DotNetNuke and I’m being introduced to the details of DNN.

item.title: Monorail on WebHost4Life
item.description: If anyone else is using hosting with WebHost4Life, I’ve gotten a Monorail site up there pretty quick using the MonoRailHttpHandlerFactory in my web.

item.title: Current things on my plate
item.description: I’m doing a good deal of stuff lately: Helping build/standardize the local .

item.title: Facebook in trouble
item.description: For those fellow Facebook users out there there is a slim chance that it might be shutdown.

item.title: South Central US Events Coming up
item.description: Via input from Chris Koenig, one of the Microsoft Developer Evangelists in my area, we got some great events coming up soon in South Central US.

item.title: Subversion web browser ($30)
item.description: Check it out at WarehouseApp Check out an example here. I plan on buying to check it out.

item.title: ReSharper now supports VB.NET
item.description: This may not be news to everyone, but ReSharper now supports VB.

item.title: Oklahoma City Developers Group Code Camp
item.description: Anyone in the Oklahoma City area on July 28th should check out the OCDG Code Camp.

item.description: I was reading Brad Abrams blog entry today about Visual Studio Island and decided to check it out.

item.description: B# (Ben Scheirman) wrote a post about how to receive this award.

item.title: Ubuntu's
item.description: LOL!! Just please go read it: [https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+bug/1] Once again, I’m not a bigot.

item.title: Visual Studio 2005 - Make new classes public by default
item.description: Thanks to a co-worker, Chris Dereadt (thanks Chris), I have finally squashed one of my pet peeves with Visual Studio 2005.

item.description: Interested information from another Linux board: “Anyways, a couple of days ago I had a major hard drive crash that corrupted

item.title: Bill McCafferty is moving to Monorail
item.description: Bill McCafferty, the author of NHibernate Best Practices on CodeProject, after researching many Separation of Concern Alternatives has decided to have his development team move to Monorail.

item.title: Technical Debt
item.description: "You have a piece of functionality that you need to add to your system.

item.title: GParted instead of Partition Magic
item.description: I’ve used Partition Magic for years. I unfortunately lost my bootable CD and have hesitated purchasing the software again.

item.title: Don't poke the penguin
item.description: Microsoft must really be hurting…..they just made the claim that Linux violates 235 of it’s patents.

item.title: Installing Subversion on Ubuntu 7.04 (Feisty Fawn) Server
item.description: This article covers installing subversion with the apache module so that it can be easily accessed from other systems on a public network.

item.title: Humble developers
item.description: It’s nice to know that developers of Oren’s caliber can admit that they have “stupid” moments too.

item.title: joeyDotNet - Creating reusable components in Monorail
item.description: I found an excellent post by joeyDotNet on how to start porting over ASP.

item.description: This past Monday I attended the ADNUG meeting in the Microsoft Technology center building in Austin, TX.

item.title: Visual Studio 2005 Web Application Projects download
item.description: UPDATE: This feature is included in the Visual Studio 2005 Service Pack 1

item.title: For anyone beginning NHibernate
item.description: I finally have a side project I’m going to use NHibernate on and blog about my experiences.

item.title: Database unit testing
item.description: Question: How do you handle database unit testing? My current position: We utilize a template pattern via a base Test Fixture and start the transaction and roll it back at TearDown.

item.title: Legacy Code
item.description: I recently read Jeremy Miller’s post on his thoughts about Legacy Code.

item.title: And we're off
item.description: LosTechies.com is up and running. This is the central posting grounds for a good number of Agile .

item.title: Ubuntu Linux offers Windows migration tool.
item.description: The newest version of Ubuntu 7.04 BETA is offering a new Windows migration tool.

item.title: For anyone starting to use NHibernate
item.description: A good blog entryby Oren (Ayende) about Nullable types and how NHibernate handles them.

item.title: Orcas - Extension Methods
item.description: Again, I was reading Guthrie’s blog and found his post on Extension Methods

item.title: Orcas - First try
item.description: After reading a post by Scott Guthrie on the new C# Orcas language features I decided to download Orcas and try them out.

item.title: Some good finds lately
item.description: An Open Letter to Scott Guthrie Free ASP.NET 2.0, LINQ, AJAX, etc.

item.title: Reminder - How to enable syntax highlighting for HTML files with non-standard extensions
item.description: If you are using VS2005, you can also go to Tools->Options->Text Editor->File Extension and associate the file extension with the HTML Editor.

item.description: I was reading an blog entry by Ayende Rahien (aka Oren Eini) again, “Entities, Services and what goes between them…” and one of his commenters asked the following question:

item.title: Google source code hosting
item.description: If you don’t want to host your own sourcecode on your own Subversion server like I was doing, check this out.

item.title: Coding Structure and Standards
item.description: Today I had a good conversation with a colleague about software structure and coding standards.

item.title: Windows Mobile 6
item.description: Microsoft announced the release of Windows Mobile 6 will during the second quarter of 2007.

item.title: The end user
item.description: I was recently speaking with a co-worker about how some projects get into a Death March because the development team never had the opportunity to speak with/see the end user.

item.title: Thoughts about reading computer books
item.description: I have been coding ASP.NET for about 4 years now. I had a co-worker bring a book to work titled “Essential ASP.

item.title: 2007 Jolt Award Finalists
item.description: The finalists The winners are announced at SD West 2007.

item.title: Free Agile Project Management Tools
item.description: I think it is awesome when a company like TargetProcess, who makes the BEST agile management tool out there, creates a site to allow new users/companies to use a free alternative.

item.title: A good read about perception and old vs. new methodologies
item.description: I have had personal experience with trying to migrate a team over to Agile methodologies and this article.

item.title: Austin .NET Users Group (ADNUG) 2007 Code Camp
item.description: If you want to increase your .NET knowledge you should plan on attending (if you’re in the Central Texas area)

item.title: Microsoft Web Application Stress (WAS)
item.description: Microsoft’s Web Application Stress Tool provides an easy way to simulate large numbers of users against your Web application.

item.title: The Anti-team
item.description: Jeremy Miller of CodeBetter have two good posts about the the anti team.

item.title: EXCELLENT Post by Jeremy Miller of CodeBetter.com
item.description: READ!! Any developer who is interested in making their code better should read the post at the link above.

item.title: Pragmatic Programmer by Hunt/Thomas
item.description: A good introduction to Agile methodologies and basic OO best practices.

item.title: Code Complete by Steve McConnell
item.description: One of the BEST centralized points of programming knowledge. Complete Complete by Steve McConnell

item.title: Law of Demeter (Principle of Least Knowledge)
item.description: I was reading up on the Law of Demeter again after reading Jeremy Miller’s post about orthogonality (good post by the way).

item.description: The following comics are by Raj (Anil Hemrajani), the new author of Agile Development with Spring, Hibernate and Eclipse.

item.description: Found this blog entry showing you how to take all images on a site and put them into a tornado.

item.description: For those developers who can’t design very well (like myself), here is a link to a free design site:

item.description: Create a label (i.e., Saved) Apply that label to your email.

item.description: Via a post, a free tool, personal edition, to develop Ruby and Ruby on Rails in Visual Studio 2005.

item.title: The D Programming Language
item.description: Slashdot has posted a new article about the D programming language.

item.title: WinSnap
item.description: I’ve used a few screen shot capturing tools and have even used the famous Alt-PrintScreen.

item.title: Jeremy Miller goes into Java land
item.description: Jeremy Miller of CodeBetter.com recently posted about his experiences with Java.

item.title: Interface Segregation Principle (ISP)
item.description: The Interface Segregation Principle (ISP) - CLIENTS SHOULD NOT BE FORCED TO DEPEND UPON INTERFACES THAT THEY DO NOT USE *This is the definition straight out of the Object Mentor article book.

item.title: Agile Principles, Patterns and Practices in C# book
item.description: I had purchased the “Agile Principles, Patterns, and Practices in C#” (PPP) book by Robert C.

item.title: A very good introduction to TDD, NHibernate, DDD, NUnit
item.description: I just recently finished reading the “A Journey with Domain Driven Design (and NHibernate)” series on Ben Scheirman’s blog.

item.title: Castle Project
item.description: I’m currently studying up on the Castle Project. It is currently scheduled for release in January of 2007, but the public API code is pretty much complete; it won’t change.

item.title: Programming Fonts
item.description: Free downloadable programming fonts created by programmers for programmers. A cool site.

item.title: System.Object.GetHashCode()
item.description: After reading Ayende’s blog about GetHashCode file about the pitfalls of GetHashCode.

item.title: Resharper Templates
item.description: I recently read a good post by Joey.NETTemplates. Worth the read if you are a ReSharperuser or are looking for a development tool to speed up your process.
